title: Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam
tagline: Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Mar Thoma Cheriyapally is a Jacobite Syrian Christian Church Church located in Kothamangalam town of Ernakulam district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Mar Thoma Cheriyapally is a Jacobite Syrian Christian Church Church located in Kothamangalam town of Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. The church is believed to have been established in 1455 by 18 families who separated from Marth Maryam Valiyapally. The church is famous for the feast of Mor Baselios Yeldo, a Syriac Jacobite saint who preached in Kothamangalam. After his death, he was entombed in the altar room of this church.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam
topPlaces.description: Martha Mariyam Cathedral or St. Mary's Church is a prominent Valiyapally (Principal Church) of the Syriac Orthodox Church situated in Kothamangalam town in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India.

image.alt: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam

topPlaces.name: Thrikkariyoor
topPlaces.description: Thrikkariyoor is a village located in the Nellikuzhi panchayat of Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is located near the village.

topPlaces.name: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is located in the town of Kothamangalam in Ernakulam district, on the northern bank of Kothayar river, a tributary of Muvattupuzha river. It is believed that this temple is one of the 108 Shiva temples of Kerala and is considered to be the last temple installed by sage Parasurama dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Kottappady
topPlaces.description: Kottappady is a village in Kothamangalam Taluk in Ernakulam district of Kerala State, India. It belongs to Central Kerala Division.

topPlaces.name: Venduvazhy
topPlaces.description: Venduvazhy is a village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. The municipal area is Kothamangalam.

topPlaces.name: Kothamangalam, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Kothamangalam ([koːd̪ɐmɐŋgɐlɐm]) is a municipality at the foot of the Western Ghats in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. It is located 42 km (26.1 mi) east of the district collectorate in Thrikkakara and about 218 km (135.5 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Pezhakkappilly
topPlaces.description: Pezhakkappilly is a suburb of Muvattupuzha town in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India, to the northwest of the town of Muvattupuzha. Its main junctions are Paipra Kavala, Sabine Junction, Pallichirangara, and Pallipady.

topPlaces.name: Varappetty
topPlaces.description: Varappetty is a village in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. Varappetty is 7 km from Kothamangalam along Kothamangalam-Vazhakulam Road, 6 km from Muvattupuzha along Puthuppady-Oonnukal Road and 12 km from Vazhakulam in Ernakulam District.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam?
faq.a: Stays in Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam?
faq.a: The top attractions are St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam, Thrikkariyoor, Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple, Kottappady, Venduvazhy.
